rulings.Case Title: BOEHLKE SPARRER COMMUNICATIONS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. MATRIX RENTAL SOLUTIONS, A NEW YORK CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award [Ccp 1285 Et Seq.]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2025, Line 1 PETITIONER BOEHLKE SPARRER COMMUNICATIONS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award [Ccp 1285 Et Seq.] The unopposed petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: EVOLUTION CAPITAL PARTNERS III LLC, A DELAWARE VS. ORACLE CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Msj Materials
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2025, Line 2 DEFENDANT ORACLE CORPORATION, FACQUISITION CORP.'S Motion To Seal MSJ Materials Defendant's motion to seal portions of the moving, opposing, and reply papers related to plaintiff's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication, is granted. A court can seal a record only if: "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (CRC 2.550(d).) Here, there is an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access. The enforcement of a binding contractual obligation not to disclose serves such an interest. (NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1222, fn. 46.) In addition, the protection of confidential business and financial information constitutes an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access. Here, the Equity Purchase Agreement, Escrow Agreement, and third-party Statement of Work ("SOW"), which incorporates the third-party's Security Requirements, contain confidentiality clauses. (MSJ Compendium Ex. 1, section 5.4; Ex. 2, section 12(h).) Those instruments also contain confidential financial information. A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced without the sealing order. Disclosure of the confidential information will adversely affect defendant's competitive abilities and perhaps subject defendant to litigation with the third-party. The proposed redactions are narrowly tailored. Unlike the prior motions to seal, defendant's application is narrow and no longer seeks to seal garden variety notice, indemnity, and choice of law provisions. Lastly, there are no less restrictive means to achieve the overriding interest. The fact that plaintiff revealed some of the financial information in the complaint does not necessarily bar the court from sealing the adduced documents. Defendant shall prepare a proposed order setting out the above verbatim and specifically listing the documents to be sealed pursuant to this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT MASJEDIAN VS. PATRICIA GUERRERO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2025, Line 3, DEFENDANT MICHELLE CURRAN, ADMINISTRATIVE DIRECTOR, JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA (SUED AS SHELLEY CURRAN) DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendant Michelle Curran's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff Albert Masjedian asserts a single cause of action against Curran asking for prospective injunctive relief declaring void court orders in Los Angeles Superior Court Case No. BC593351 and California State Bar Court case number 09-0-10207/10-0-08540-PEM. The court sustains the demurrer because this court lacks the fundamental authority to enter an order voiding an order of another court. (CCP 430.10.) With respect to an order of the Los Angeles Superior Court, only a court exercising superior jurisdiction may void or overrule it. (Cf. Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court of Santa Clara County (1962) 57 Cal.2d 450, 455.) With respect to a State Bar Court matter, such decisions may be reviewed pursuant to Rule of Court 9.13 by petition to the Supreme Court by the licensee. A new lawsuit in this court is not an avenue of review. The court also sustains the demurrer because it fails to state a claim for relief under the Fourteenth Amendment. Plaintiff's opposing papers do not identify any facts he could allege that would cure these defects. Accordingly, the court does not grant leave to amend. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WILLIAM DRAKE VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2025, Line 5, PLAINTIFF PAUL DRAKE'S, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Sanctions The court grants in part and denies in part plaintiff Paul William Drake's motion to compel further responses to his request for production of documents, set one, from defendant American Honda Motor Co. (AHM). The court orders AHM to pay $2500 in sanctions to Drake within 10 court days of this order. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.)
